    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote">{`In this part, you will learn how to create new `}<em parentName="p">{`Notes`}</em>{` package,

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "generate-files-skeleton"
    }}>{`Generate Files Skeleton`}</h2>
    <p>{`The simple way to initialize a app package is using MetaFox AdminCP.
Visit AdminCP at "/admincp/app" on your browser, click on Create App button and then follow the Wizard to create your first app`}</p>
    <p>{`Let's start by initialize the schema structure`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "generate-database-schema"

    <p>{`Open terminal and run the following command`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`# create migration file
php artisan package:make-migration company/note create_note_schema
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Go to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Database/Migrations`}</inlineCode>{` directory, open a PHP file`}</p>
    <p>{`You'll see a class with two methods `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`up`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`down`}</inlineCode>{`. Next step, we will add some code to create table schema in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`up()`}</inlineCode>{` method and destroy table schema to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`down()`}</inlineCode>{` method.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class CreateBlogTables extends Migration
{
    public function up()
    {
        if (!Schema::hasTable('notes')) {
            Schema::create('notes', function (Blueprint $table) {
                // Create table structure.
            });
        }
    }

    public function down()
    {
        Schema::dropIfExists('notes');
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "code-generator"

    <p>{`To generate an example code to a `}<em parentName="p">{`Resource`}</em>{`, access `}<strong parentName="p">{`AdminCP > Installed > Apps`}</strong>{` or use the direct URL `}<em parentName="p">{`/admin/apps`}</em>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "type"
    }}>{`Type`}</h3>
    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` is `}<em parentName="p">{`Content`}</em>{`, you need to implement:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`interface: `}<strong parentName="li">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\Content`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`trait: `}<strong parentName="li">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\HasContent`}</strong>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` is `}<em parentName="p">{`User`}</em>{`, you need to implement:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`interface: `}<strong parentName="li">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\User`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`trait: `}<strong parentName="li">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\HasUser`}</strong>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "define-model-userowner"
    }}>{`Define Model User/Owner`}</h3>
    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` has `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_type`}</inlineCode>{`, use trait `}<strong parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Eloquent\\Model\\HasUserMorph`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` has `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_type`}</inlineCode>{`, use trait `}<strong parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Eloquent\\Model\\HasOwnerMorph`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "privacy"
    }}>{`Privacy`}</h3>
    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` has privacy and MUST has custom privacy (if not, skip this step), you need to implement:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`interface: `}<strong parentName="li">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\Eloquent\\Appends\\Contracts\\AppendPrivacyList`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`trait: `}<strong parentName="li">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\Eloquent\\Appends\\AppendPrivacyListTrait`}</strong>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This will give `}<em parentName="p">{`model`}</em>{` two methods, `}<strong parentName="p">{`setPrivacyListAttribute`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`getPrivacyListAttribute`}</strong>{`, to set/get privacy list. For example:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"

$blog->setPrivacyListAttribute([1, 2, 3]);
var_dump($blog->getPrivacyListAttribute()); // print 1, 2, 3. If privacy != 4, it will return null.
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "create-feed"
    }}>{`Create Feed`}</h3>
    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` is `}<em parentName="p">{`Content`}</em>{`, and `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` can create Feed, you will need to implement interface `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\ActivityFeedSource`}</inlineCode></p>
    <p>{`Method `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`_toActivityFeed_`}</inlineCode>{` will return a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`_FeedAction_`}</inlineCode>{` instance, this is feed data before creating a feed. Making sure `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`type_id`}</inlineCode>{` is correct (see `}<em parentName="p">{`Settings`}</em>{`)`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "stream"

    <p>{`If `}<em parentName="p">{`Model`}</em>{` can create `}<em parentName="p">{`Resource Stream`}</em>{` (using for browsing), you can implement the interface `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\HasResourceStream`}</inlineCode></p>
    <p>{`Firstly, we will define `}<em parentName="p">{`Blog`}</em>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`PrivacyStream`}</inlineCode>{` class as below:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"

use Illuminate\\Database\\Eloquent\\Model;

class PrivacyStream extends Model
{
    protected $table = 'blog_privacy_streams';

}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Next, in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Blog`}</inlineCode>{` model class, we define its relation:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"

{

}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Blog`}</inlineCode>{` Model class now looks like this:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\Content;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Eloquent\\Model\\HasUserMorph;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Eloquent\\Model\\HasOwnerMorph;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\ActivityFeedSource;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\HasResourceStream;

class Blog extends Model implements Content, ActivityFeedSource, AppendPrivacyList, HasResourceStream;
{
    use HasContent;
    use HasUserMorph;
    use HasOwnerMorph;
    use AppendPrivacyListTrait;

    public const ENTITY_TYPE = 'blog'; // Always define ENTITY_TYPE.

    public function toActivityFeed(): FeedAction
    {
        return new FeedAction([
            'user_id'    => $this->userId(),
            'user_type'  => $this->userType(),
            'owner_id'   => $this->ownerId(),
            'owner_type' => $this->ownerType(),
            'item_id'    => $this->entityId(),
            'item_type'  => $this->entityType(),
            'type_id'    => 'blog_created',
            'privacy'    => $this->privacy,
        ]);
    }

    public function privacy_streams(): HasMany
    {
        return $this->hasMany(PrivacyStream::class, 'item_id', 'id');
    }
}

`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "use-entity-name"

    <p>{`By default, Laravel will use the classname `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Blog\\Models\\Blog`}</inlineCode>{`. To use `}<strong parentName="p">{`blog`}</strong>{` only, go to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Providers/PackageServiceProvider`}</inlineCode>{` and define in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`boot`}</inlineCode>{` method`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`public function boot()
{
     Relation::morphMap([
        Blog::ENTITY_TYPE => Blog::class,
    ]);
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "model-observer"
    }}>{`Model Observer`}</h2>
    <p>{`You can define Model Observer in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`boot`}</inlineCode>{` method of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Providers/PackageServiceProvider`}</inlineCode>{` class.`}</p>
    <p>{`Note if model is `}<em parentName="p">{`User`}</em>{` or `}<em parentName="p">{`Content`}</em>{` Type, you must register with `}<em parentName="p">{`global`}</em>{` observer. Depending on abilities of Interface, Model will listen differently with platform process.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class BlogServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    public function boot()
    {
         Blog::observe([
            \\MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\EloquentModelObserver::class, // Global observer.
            \\MetaFox\\Blog\\Observers\\BlogObserver::class, // Your own blog observer.
         ]);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "setting-to-create-feed"
    }}>{`Setting to create Feed`}</h3>
    <p>{`To make a `}<em parentName="p">{`Content`}</em>{` Model have ability to create Feed, you can define `}<em parentName="p">{`activity type`}</em>{` as below:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class PackageSettingListener extends BasePackageSettingListener
{
    // ....

    public function getActivityTypes(): array
    {
        return [
            [
                'type'            => 'blog_created', // This is the name of action.
                'module_id'       => $this->getPackageName(),
                'is_active'       => true,
                'title'           => 'Blog Activity Type',
                'description'     => 'Blog Activity Type Setting',
                'is_system'       => 0,
                'can_comment'     => true, // Ability to do actions on feed.
                'can_like'        => true,
                'can_share'       => true,
                'can_edit'        => true,
                'can_create_feed' => true,
                'can_put_stream'  => true,
            ],
        ];
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "add-resource-to-activity-form"
    }}>{`Add Resource to Activity Form`}</h3>
    <p>{`In MetaFox, you can add default settings quickly when adding a `}<em parentName="p">{`resource`}</em>{` to Activity Feed Form.`}</p>

    <h3 {...{

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "setting-user-permissions"
    }}>{`Setting User Permissions`}</h3>
    <p>{`In MetaFox, we assign permissions to role in `}<em parentName="p">{`tree`}</em>{`. For example, if you define a certain permission with a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED`}</inlineCode>{` role, then users in higher roles will have such permission also.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class PackageSettingListener extends BasePackageSettingListener
{
    // ....
    public function getUserPermissions(): array
    {
        return [
            Blog::ENTITY_TYPE     => [
                'view'     => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
                'create'   => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
                'update'   => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
                'delete'   => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
                'moderate' => UserRole::LEVEL_STAFF,
                'feature'  => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
                'approve'  => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
                'sponsor'  => UserRole::LEVEL_REGISTERED,
            ],
        ];
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`If you just want to assign a permission to a specific role:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
return [
    Blog::ENTITY_TYPE     => [
        'specific-permission' => [UserRole::STAFF_USER]
    ],
];

`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "site-setting"
    }}>{`Site Setting`}</h3>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "setting-user-privacy"
    }}>{`Setting User Privacy`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can define following methods for User Privacy`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`getUserPrivacy`}</inlineCode>{`: define all profile settings.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`getUserPrivacyResource`}</inlineCode>{`: define all settings (defined in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`getUserPrivacy`}</inlineCode>{` methods) used by resources. For example: you create a setting `}<em parentName="li">{`Blog-A`}</em>{`, then you want to apply it to page, group v.v... you have to define them in this method only. And, you can set its phrases too.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`getDefaultPrivacy`}</inlineCode>{`: define default privacy of a `}<em parentName="li">{`resource`}</em>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`getProfileMenu`}</inlineCode>{`: define sections which can be viewed on user profile.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
class PackageSettingListener extends BasePackageSettingListener
{
    // ....

    public function getUserPrivacy(): array
    {
        return [
            'blog.share_blogs'       => [
                'phrase'  => 'blog.phrase.user_privacy.who_can_share_blogs',
                'default' => MetaFoxPrivacy::EVERYONE,
            ],
            'blog.view_browse_blogs' => [
                'phrase'  => 'blog.phrase.user_privacy.who_can_view_blogs',
                'default' => MetaFoxPrivacy::EVERYONE,
            ],
        ];
    }

    public function getUserPrivacyResource(): array
    {
        return [
            'page'  => [
                'blog.share_blogs',
                'blog.view_browse_blogs',
                'custom_seting' => 'custom_setting_phrase',
            ],
            'group' => [
                'blog.share_blogs',
            ],
        ];
    }

    public function getDefaultPrivacy()
    {
        return [
            Blog::ENTITY_TYPE => [
                'phrase'  => 'blog.phrase.blogs',
                'default' => MetaFoxPrivacy::EVERYONE,
            ],
            // set for more resources.
            // ...
        ];
    }

    public function getProfileMenu(): array
    {
        return [
            Blog::ENTITY_TYPE => [
                'phrase'  => 'blog.phrase.blogs',
                'default' => MetaFoxPrivacy::EVERYONE,
            ],
            // set for more resources.
            // ...
        ];
    }
}

`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "repository"
    }}>{`Repository`}</h2>
    <p><em parentName="p">{`Repository`}</em>{` can be extended from the abstract class `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Platform\\Repositories\\AbstractRepository`}</inlineCode>{` if needed. But a `}<em parentName="p">{`repository`}</em>{` is always required to implement the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Repository`}</inlineCode>{` interface.`}</p>
    <p>{`For example, in the `}<em parentName="p">{`Blog`}</em>{` app:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Location for `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`repository`}</inlineCode>{` interfaces: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`company/blog/src/Repositories`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Location for `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`repository`}</inlineCode>{` class: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`company/blog/src/Repositories/Eloquent`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`namespace MetaFox\\Blog\\Repositories\\Eloquent;

use MetaFox\\Platform\\Repositories\\AbstractRepository;
use MetaFox\\Blog\\Repositories\\BlogRepositoryInterface;

class BlogRepository extends AbstractRepository implements BlogRepositoryInterface
{
    public function model(): string
    {
        return Blog::class;
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Go to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Providers/BlogServiceProvider`}</inlineCode>{` class and add some code to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`register`}</inlineCode>{` method`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`use MetaFox\\Blog\\Repositories\\BlogRepositoryInterface;
use MetaFox\\Blog\\Repositories\\Eloquent\\BlogRepository;

class BlogServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    public function register()
    {
         $this->app->bind(BlogRepositoryInterface::class, BlogRepository::class);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`For detail, please read about `}<em parentName="p">{`Laravel Repository`}</em>{` pattern`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "translation"
    }}>{`Translation`}</h2>
    <p>{`Each app will have a folder `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/resources/lang`}</inlineCode>{` for its language pack. For example, in the `}<em parentName="p">{`Blog`}</em>{` app:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`company/
    blog/
        resources/
            lang/
                en/
                    phrase.php
`}</code></pre>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote">{`Note: JSON file hasn't been supported yet.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <p>{`Language phrases can be added or modified in the respective `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`phrase.php`}</inlineCode>{` file. For example, to add/modify English phrases, we will update the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/resources/lang/en/phrase.php`}</inlineCode>{` file`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`return [
    'example_text' => 'Example text',
    'group' => [
        'example_text' => 'Group example text',
    ]
];

`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`After changing language phrases in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`phrase.php`}</inlineCode>{` file, you can run the following `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`php artisan`}</inlineCode>{` command to update:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`php artisan MetaFox:update
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "usage"
    }}>{`Usage`}</h3>
    <p>{`To apply translation for phrases, call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`_p`}</inlineCode>{` method with phrase variable. Phrase variable is the combination of `}<em parentName="p">{`app name`}</em>{`, `}<em parentName="p">{`file name`}</em>{` and `}<em parentName="p">{`phrase key (+ n key level)`}</em>{`. Use dots (.) as separators. Here are some examples to print above `}<em parentName="p">{`Blog`}</em>{` phrases`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`echo __p('blog.phrase.example_text'); // it will print 'Example text'.
echo __p('blog.phrase.group.example_text'); // it will print 'Group Example text'.
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "resource"
    }}>{`Resource`}</h2>
    <p>{`Go to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/src/Http/Resources/v1/Blog`}</inlineCode>{`, you will see following classes:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogDetail`}</inlineCode>{`: is a resource when viewing `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Blog`}</inlineCode>{` detail.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogEmbed`}</inlineCode>{`: is an embedded Blog object when viewing feed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogEmbedCollection`}</inlineCode>{`: list of `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogEmbed`}</inlineCode>{` resources.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogItem`}</inlineCode>{`: is a resource when viewing listing.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogItemCollection`}</inlineCode>{`: is a list of `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`BlogItem`}</inlineCode>{` resources.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Example for `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`BlogDetail`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <p>{`We support 3 trait Helpers:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`HasStatistic`}</inlineCode>{`: support method `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`getStatistic()`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`HasExtra`}</inlineCode>{`: support method `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`getExtra()`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`HasFeedParam`}</inlineCode>{`: support method `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`getFeedParams()`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Http\\Resources\\HasExtra;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Http\\Resources\\HasFeedParam;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Traits\\Http\\Resources\\HasStatistic;
use Illuminate\\Http\\Resources\\Json\\JsonResource;

class BlogDetail extends JsonResource
{
    use HasExtra;
    use HasStatistic;
    use HasFeedParam;

    public function toArray($request)
    {
        'id'                => $this->resource->id,
        'module_name'       => $this->resource->entityType(),
        'resource_name'     => $this->resource->entityType(),

        // Other attributes.
        'statistic'         => $this->getStatistic(),
        'extra'             => $this->getExtra(),
        'feed_param'        => $this->getFeedParams(),

        // Custom attributes.
        'custom-attribute'  => 'abc'
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "form-request"
    }}>{`Form Request`}</h2>
    <p>{`Go to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/src/Http/Requests/v1/Blog`}</inlineCode>{`, you will see some default `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`FormRequest`}</inlineCode>{` classes such as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`IndexRequest`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`StoreRequest`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`UpdateRequest`}</inlineCode></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
class StoreRequest extends FormRequest
{
    // Form rules.
    public function rules()
    {
        return [
            'post_type'         => ['required'],
            'privacy'           => ['sometimes', new PrivacyRule()],
            'post_as_parent'    => ['sometimes', 'exists:user_entities,id'],
            'tagged_friends'    => ['sometimes', 'array'],
            'location'          => [
                'sometimes', 'array',
            ],
            'location.address'  => 'string',
            'location.lat'      => 'numeric',
            'location.lng'      => 'numeric',
        ];
    }

    // If you want to custom attributes after validated successfully, use this method.
    public function validated()
    {
        $data = parent::validated();

        if (empty($data['post_as_parent'])) {
            $data['post_as_parent'] = Auth::id();
        }

        if (!isset($data['privacy'])) {
            $data['privacy'] = MetaFoxPrivacy::EVERYONE;
        }

        return $data;
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "api"
    }}>{`API`}</h2>
    <p>{`Go to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Http/Controllers/Api`}</inlineCode>{`, you will see some `}<em parentName="p">{`Controller`}</em>{` classes. They are extended from `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`GatewayController`}</inlineCode>{` classes for multiple API versions.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
namespace MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api;

use MetaFox\\Platform\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\GatewayController;

/**
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Api Gateway
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *
 * This class solve api versioning problem.
 * DO NOT IMPLEMENT ACTION HERE.
 */

/**
 * Class CategoryController
 * @package MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api
 */
class BlogController extends GatewayController
{
    protected $controllers = [
        'v1'   => v1\\BlogController::class,
        // Define many Controllers as you want here.
    ];

    // DO NOT IMPLEMENT ACTION HERE.
}

`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`For each version, example `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v1`}</inlineCode>{`, you can create a folder `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v1`}</inlineCode>{` and create a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`BlogController`}</inlineCode>{` class in it`}</p>
    <p>{`You MUST extend from Platform's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ApiController`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`If you need to create a `}<em parentName="p">{`Repository`}</em>{` instance, you have to create by interface as below`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`namespace MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\v1;

use MetaFox\\Platform\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\ApiController;
use MetaFox\\Blog\\Repositories\\BlogRepositoryInterface;
use MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Requests\\v1\\Blog\\IndexRequest;
use MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Resources\\v1\\Blog\\BlogItemCollection;

class BlogController extends ApiController
{
    public $repository;

    public function __construct(BlogRepositoryInterface $repository)
    {
        $this->repository = $repository;
    }

    public function store(StoreRequest $request)
    {
        // Use this method to get form data.
        $params = $request->validated();

        // Use this to get context user. Note: making sure whether this action is authenticated or not. If not, do not use this.
        $user = Auth::user();

        // Post to where?
        $owner = $params['post_as_parent'] ? UserEntity::getById($params['post_as_parent'])->detail : $user;

        // Best practice: when creating a Content, should always define data => post from who => post to who.
        $blog = $this->repository->createBlog($params, $user, $owner);

        // After get data, populate it into Resource.
        return new Detail($blog);
    }
}

`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "scope"
    }}>{`Scope`}</h2>
    <p>{`If you want to query `}<em parentName="p">{`Blog`}</em>{` by custom query, please do not write any custom query inside `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Blog`}</inlineCode>{` Model class.`}</p>
    <p>{`Instead, go to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Scopes`}</inlineCode>{` and create `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ExampleScope`}</inlineCode>{` class as below`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
use Illuminate\\Database\\Eloquent\\Scope;
use Illuminate\\Database\\Eloquent\\Builder;
use Illuminate\\Database\\Eloquent\\Model;

class ExampleScope implements BaseScope
{
    private $example;

    public function setExample($data)
    {
        $this->example = $data;
    }

    public function getExample()
    {
        return $this->example;
    }

    public function apply(Builder $builder, Model $model)
    {
        $builder->where('example_column', '=', $this->getExample());
    }
}

`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "usage-1"
    }}>{`Usage`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
$yourScope = new ExampleScope();
$yourScope->setExample('123');

$blog = new Blog();
$query = $blog->newQuery()->addScope($yourScope);
$query->limit(1);
$data = $query->get();

`}</code></pre>

    <h4 {...{
      "id": "customize-platform-scopes"
    }}>{`Customize platform scopes`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`If you want to override a certain platform Scope, for example, customize the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`SortScope`}</inlineCode>{` in the `}<em parentName="li">{`Photo`}</em>{` app, you can add the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`SortScope`}</inlineCode>{` class as below`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`namespace MetaFox\\Photo\\Support\\Browse\\Scopes\\Album;

class SortScope extends \\MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\Browse\\Scopes\\SortScope
{
    public const SORT_MOST_PHOTO = 'most_photo';

    public function apply(Builder $builder, Model $model)
    {
        parent::apply($builder, $model);

        $table = $model->getTable();
        $sort = $this->getSort();
        $sortType = $this->getSortType();

        if ($sort == self::SORT_MOST_PHOTO) {
            $builder->orderBy($this->alias($table, 'total_photo'), $sortType);
            $builder->orderBy($this->alias($table, 'id'), $sortType);
        }
    }
}

`}</code></pre>

        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
$sortScope = new  \\MetaFox\\Photo\\Support\\Browse\\Scopes\\Album\\SortScope();
$sortScope->setSort('recent');
`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "privacy-1"
    }}>{`Privacy`}</h2>
    <p>{`To check privacy, you should rely on the following database tables:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="p">{`core_privacy`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`Everyone`}</em>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode>{` is `}<strong parentName="li">{`1`}</strong>{`. It's generic privacy. You can use it directly as a constant.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`Friends`}</em>{`: each User will have a privacy with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy_type = user_friends`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy = 1`}</inlineCode></li>
          <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`Only Me`}</em>{`: each User will have a privacy with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy_type = user_private`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy = 3`}</inlineCode></li>
          <li parentName="ul"><em parentName="li">{`Custom Privacy`}</em>{`: an User can have unlimited custom privacy. Example: if you created a `}<em parentName="li">{`friend list`}</em>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`id = 1`}</inlineCode>{`, you will have a `}<em parentName="li">{`Privacy`}</em>{` record with: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`item_id = 1, item_type = friend_list, privacy = 4, privacy_type = user_friend_list`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="p">{`core_privacy_members`}</inlineCode>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode></p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`User B adds a certain item with `}<em parentName="li">{`Friends`}</em>{` privacy. To verify if a user A (having `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`user_id`}</inlineCode>{` = 1 for example) can see that item or not:`}
            <ul parentName="li">
              <li parentName="ul">{`Query `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode>{` of User B with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`user_private = user_friends`}</inlineCode>{`. For example, we got the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode>{` = 100. Then, if we have record with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`user_id = 1`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`privacy_id = 100`}</inlineCode>{` => User A can view that item`}</li>
            </ul>
          </li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Note: owner of a privacy always has a record in this table.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="p">{`core_privacy_streams`}</inlineCode>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`item_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`item_type`}</inlineCode></p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Stored privacy of items.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`If an item has `}<em parentName="li">{`X`}</em>{` privacy custom friend list, it will have `}<em parentName="li">{`X`}</em>{` stream records.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="p">{`[app_name]_privacy_streams`}</inlineCode>{`: your app stream. Need to replace the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`[app_name]`}</inlineCode>{` with your actual app name, such as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`blog_privacy_streams`}</inlineCode>{`. It's a children of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`core_privacy_streams`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
      </li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "easier-way-browse-stream"
    }}>{`Easier way: browse stream.`}</h3>

    <p>{`It's user privacy setting.`}</p>

`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "policy"
    }}>{`Policy`}</h2>
    <p><em parentName="p">{`Policy`}</em>{` is similar to `}<em parentName="p">{`Access Control`}</em>{` in `}<strong parentName="p">{`phpFox V4`}</strong>{`. Going to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`company/blog/src/Policies`}</inlineCode>{`, you will see some `}<em parentName="p">{`Policy`}</em>{` classes, such as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`BlogPolicy`}</inlineCode>{` class`}</p>
    <p>{`For example: to check if a user can create Blog, we will combine `}<em parentName="p">{`Permission`}</em>{`, `}<em parentName="p">{`Privacy`}</em>{`, `}<em parentName="p">{`User Privacy`}</em>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`And, always check 5 levels as below`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`use MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\Facades\\PrivacyPolicy;
use MetaFox\\User\\Support\\Facades\\UserPrivacy;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Facades\\Settings;
use MetaFox\\Blog\\Models\\Blog;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\Content as Resource;
use MetaFox\\Platform\\Contracts\\User as User;

class BlogPolicy
{
    protected string $type = Blog::class;

    public function view(User $user, Content $resource) {

        // 1. Check role/permission.
        if (!$user->hasPermissionTo('blog.view')) {
            return false;
        }

        $owner = $resource->owner;

        // 2. Check can view on owner.
        if (!PrivacyPolicy::checkPermissionOwner($user, $owner)) {
            return false;
        }

        // 3. Check owner user privacy setting of resource. Example: can_view_blog_on_owner
        if (UserPrivacy::hasAccess($user->entityId(), $owner->entityId(), 'blog.can_view_blog_on_owner') == false) {
            return false;
        }

        // 4. Check resource setting.
        if ($resource->is_pending == true) {
            if (Settings::get('blog.can_view_pending') == false) {
                return false;
            }
        }

        // 4.1 Or setting can be disable resource privacy.
        if (Settings::get('blog.privacy_enabled') == true) {
            return true;
        }

        // 5. Check can view on resource.
        if (PrivacyPolicy::checkPermission($user, $resource) == false) {
            return false;
        }

        return true;
    }
}
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "policy-1"
    }}>{`Policy`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class PhotoPolicy
{
 /**
     * Determine whether the user can feature the model.
     *
     * @param User     $user
     * @param resource $resource
     * @param int|null $featureUpdateValue
     *
     * @return bool
     */
    public function feature(User $user, Resource $resource, int $featureUpdateValue = null): bool
    {
        if ($resource instanceof HasFeature) {
            if ($featureUpdateValue != null) {
                if ($featureUpdateValue == $resource->is_featured) {
                    return false;
                }
            }

            if ($user->hasPermissionTo('photo.feature')) {
                return true;
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

     /**
     * Determine whether the user can sponsor the model.
     *
     * @param User     $user
     * @param resource $resource
     * @param int|null $sponsorUpdateValue
     *
     * @return bool
     */
    public function sponsor(User $user, Resource $resource, int $sponsorUpdateValue = null): bool
    {
        if ($resource instanceof HasSponsor) {
            if ($sponsorUpdateValue != null) {
                if ($sponsorUpdateValue == $resource->is_sponsor) {
                    return false;
                }
            }

            if ($user->hasPermissionTo('photo.sponsor')) {
                return true;
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

    /**
     * Determine whether the user can approve the model.
     *
     * @param User          $user
     * @param resource|null $resource $resource
     *
     * @return bool
     */
    public function approve(User $user, Resource $resource = null): bool
    {
        if ($resource instanceof HasApprove) {
            if (Photo::IS_APPROVED == $resource->is_approved) {
                return false;
            }
        }

        if (!$user->hasPermissionTo('photo.approve')) {
            return false;
        }

        return true;
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
